seo.title: 100 Fine-Tuning & Custom Models resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Fine-Tuning & Custom Models resources for engineers.

content.intro: Fine-tuning shifts model behavior from general-purpose reasoning to domain-specific expertise. This guide focuses on the technical stack required to move beyond prompt engineering, covering dataset synthesis, parameter-efficient training (PEFT) workflows, and the infrastructure needed to serve custom weights in production environments.

items.title: OpenAI JSONL Formatting
items.description: Structure training data into the messages-array format required by the OpenAI Fine-Tuning API to ensure compatibility with gpt-4o-mini and gpt-3.5-turbo fine-tuning endpoints.

items.title: Hugging Face Datasets 'map' function
items.description: Utilize the .map() function with batched=True to apply tokenization and formatting scripts across large-scale datasets efficiently in memory.

items.title: Argilla Feedback Loops
items.description: Implement a human-in-the-loop interface to label and curate high-quality preference data for RLHF or DPO workflows.

items.title: Evol-Instruct Method
items.description: Use a teacher model (e.g., GPT-4) to rewrite existing instruction sets into more complex versions, increasing the depth of the training signal.

items.title: Cleanlab for Label Noise
items.description: Run Cleanlab's Datalab to automatically identify and remove outlier or mislabeled examples that can degrade fine-tuning performance.

items.title: Synthetic Data Generation with Llama-3-70B
items.description: Leverage larger open models to generate synthetic reasoning chains (Chain of Thought) for training smaller 7B or 8B models.

items.title: MinHash Deduplication
items.description: Apply MinHash and Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) to remove near-duplicate examples from your corpus to prevent overfitting on repetitive data.

items.title: Lilac Data Exploration
items.description: Use Lilac to visualize embeddings and clusters within your training set to identify gaps in domain coverage before starting a training run.

items.title: Nomic Atlas for Latent Space Analysis
items.description: Map your dataset in latent space to verify that the fine-tuning data is sufficiently distinct from the base model's pre-training distribution.

items.title: Text-to-SQL Schema Augmentation
items.description: Inject DDL statements directly into the prompt field of your training data to teach the model specific database schemas.

items.title: Unsloth for Accelerated Training
items.description: Use the Unsloth library to achieve 2x faster fine-tuning and 70% less VRAM usage for Llama-3, Mistral, and Gemma models.

items.title: QLoRA (4-bit Quantization)
items.description: Implement 4-bit NormalFloat quantization to fine-tune 70B parameter models on a single 48GB GPU (like an A6000).

items.title: Axolotl YAML Configs
items.description: Standardize experiments using Axolotl's declarative YAML configuration files to manage hyperparameters, dataset paths, and LoRA settings.

items.title: LoRA Rank (r) and Alpha Selection
items.description: Set r=16 or r=32 for most tasks; ensure lora_alpha is set to 2x the rank to maintain consistent scaling of the weights.

items.title: DeepSpeed ZeRO-3
items.description: Enable DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 for full-parameter fine-tuning to shard model states across multiple GPUs when PEFT is insufficient.

items.title: Target Modules Specification
items.description: Target all linear layers (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj) in LoRA to achieve maximum performance.

items.title: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
items.description: Use the TRL library to align models with preference pairs (chosen vs. rejected) without the complexity of PPO.

items.title: NEFTune Noise Injection
items.description: Add random noise to the embedding layer during training to improve the model's robustness and generalization to unseen prompts.

items.title: Weights & Biases (W&B) Integration
items.description: Log loss curves, GPU utilization, and gradient norms to identify training instability or vanishing gradients in real-time.

items.title: Flash Attention 2 Integration
items.description: Enable Flash Attention 2 in your training scripts to reduce memory overhead and speed up the attention mechanism by up to 3x.

items.title: LM Evaluation Harness
items.description: Run the EleutherAI Evaluation Harness across standard benchmarks (MMLU, GSM8K) to detect regressions in general reasoning.

items.title: vLLM Inference Engine
items.description: Deploy fine-tuned weights using vLLM for high-throughput serving with PagedAttention and continuous batching.

items.title: LoRAX (LoRA Exchange)
items.description: Use LoRAX to serve hundreds of different fine-tuned adapters on a single base model instance to minimize infrastructure costs.

items.title: LLM-as-a-Judge (MT-Bench)
items.description: Use GPT-4 as an automated evaluator to score your fine-tuned model's responses against a reference set of high-quality answers.

items.title: GGUF Quantization for Local Serving
items.description: Convert fine-tuned weights to GGUF format using llama.cpp to run custom models on consumer hardware or edge devices.

items.title: PromptFoo Matrix Testing
items.description: Run systematic tests comparing the base model vs. the fine-tuned model across hundreds of test cases to quantify improvement.

items.title: SafeTensors Format Conversion
items.description: Always save and distribute model weights in .safetensors format to prevent arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities associated with pickles.

items.title: Text Generation Inference (TGI)
items.description: Deploy via Hugging Face TGI for production features like dynamic batching, stop sequences, and optimized CUDA kernels.

items.title: Model Merging (Mergekit)
items.description: Use Mergekit to combine your fine-tuned LoRA weights with other domain-specific models using SLERP or TIES merging techniques.

items.title: Ollama Modelfile Deployment
items.description: Create a custom Modelfile to package your fine-tuned weights, system prompt, and parameters for easy distribution within a team.

pro_tips: Always perform a 'dry run' with 10% of your data to check if the loss is decreasing before committing to a full multi-day training run.
If fine-tuning for structured output (JSON), include the schema in the system prompt of every training example to reinforce structural constraints.
Avoid over-training; once the validation loss starts to plateau or tick upward, stop training to prevent the model from losing its general reasoning capabilities.
Use a constant learning rate scheduler with a short warmup period for LoRA; 2e-4 is a reliable starting point for most 7B-8B parameter models.
Prioritize data quality over quantity; 500 extremely high-quality, manually verified examples often outperform 50,000 noisy, machine-generated ones.
